macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Close Higher on February 24, 2026
items.summary: S&P 500 rose 0.78% to 6,891.04, Nasdaq Composite gained 1.05% to 22,863.68, and Dow Jones increased 0.76% to 49,174.81 amid investor optimism ahead of key economic data. Russell 2000 also advanced 1.2% to 2,652.33.[1][2]

items.title: Wall Street Rebounds After Monday's Decline
items.summary: US stocks recovered in a mean-reversion session following a rough Monday, with equities leading risk assets higher despite ongoing uncertainty from Supreme Court ruling on IEEPA tariffs. Gold retraced while silver, platinum, and copper advanced; US dollar and Treasuries held firm.[3]

items.title: Markets Eye Key Economic Data Amid Tariff Developments
items.summary: Investor focus shifted to upcoming economic figures after a session lacking major news, with gains attributed to optimism; Supreme Court tariff block seen reducing duties for exporters like China, India, and Brazil. Individual movers included Keysight Technologies and Immunitybio as top gainers.[2][3]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $65K Amid U.S. Tariff Announcement and $500M Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below $65,000 to around $64,300–$64,400 following U.S. President Trump's executive order imposing a 15% global tariff, triggering over $500 million in leveraged position liquidations and intense selling pressure.[1]

items.title: Vitalik Buterin Moves 428+ ETH to Stablecoins as Whales Sell During Volatility
items.summary: On-chain data shows Vitalik Buterin transferred over 428 ETH into stablecoins, while Bitdeer sold its entire 943 BTC holdings, contributing to market sentiment in 'Extreme Fear' amid peak volatility.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Prices Remain in Downtrend Despite Slight Recovery in Futures
items.summary: March Bitcoin futures are firmer but hover near recent lows, with daily charts confirming an ongoing downtrend as per technical analysis.[3]

items.title: Zac XBT Teases Major Insider Trading Revelation in Crypto for Tomorrow
items.summary: Investigator Zac XBT announced an upcoming report on significant insider trading cases in a highly profitable project, set for release tomorrow amid three days of market turbulence including Nvidia results and Bitcoin options expiry.[2]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: MIT Develops AI Framework for Multimodal Cell Biology Analysis
items.summary: Researchers at MIT created a machine-learning framework that distinguishes shared and unique data across cellular measurement modalities like transcriptomics and chromatin accessibility, aiding disease research and experiment planning.

items.title: MIT Discovers Neuron-Specific Learning Signals Resembling Machine Learning Error Signals
items.summary: A study led by Mark Harnett at MIT found that mouse brains deliver precise, vectorized instructive signals to individual neurons during learning via brain-computer interfaces, bridging neuroscience and AI model development.

items.title: NYU Abu Dhabi Professor Advances Knowledge Graphs for Reliable AI Fact-Checking
items.summary: Djellel Difallah is developing interconnected knowledge graphs using machine learning and crowdsourcing to enable AI systems to reason with verified data, improving explainability and reliability over pattern-based generation.

items.title: AI in Education Market Projected to Reach USD 136.79 Billion by 2035
items.summary: The market, valued at USD 7.05 billion in 2025, is driven by machine learning for hyper-personalized learning and intelligent tutoring; recent initiatives include OpenAI's February 2026 launch in higher education and Google’s $1B investment.

items.title: Pearson Research: AI Study Tools Boost Active Learning in College Students
items.summary: Analysis of 80 million student interactions shows AI-embedded tools in digital materials increase active reading behaviors by turning passive consumption into deeper engagement, with 97% responsible use in biology eTextbooks.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nacon Files for Insolvency Weeks Before GreedFall 2 Launch
items.summary: Publisher Nacon, behind GreedFall: The Dying World, Hell Is Us, and RoboCop: Rogue City, has filed for insolvency due to its majority shareholder's financial issues. The RPG sequel is still expected to launch on March 12, and Nacon Connect showcase proceeds on March 4.[1]

items.title: Gamevestor Launches as Europe's First Video Game Crowd-Investing Platform
items.summary: Gamevestor officially launched today, enabling players to invest directly in video games through a new European crowd-investing platform dedicated exclusively to the industry.[2]

items.title: MSU Esports Program Dominates with Top-Ranked Teams and Upcoming Championships
items.summary: Michigan State University's esports teams excel in Super Smash Bros. Ultimate, Rocket League, and Valorant, with top North American players and scholarships; they gear up for Big Esports Conference championships March 6-8.[3]

items.title: State of Gaming 2026 Report: Revenue Holds Amid Declining Downloads and Playtime
items.summary: The 2026 gaming industry report shows stable revenue despite drops in downloads and time spent, with tighter monetization, booming strategy games, higher attention costs, and emphasis on retention.[4]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Innoviz Technologies Announces Strong Q4 Earnings and 2026 Guidance
items.summary: Innoviz Technologies reported 2025 revenue of $55.1M, more than double 2024, with 23% gross margin and guides 2026 revenue to $67-73M. Key wins include supplying LiDAR for Daimler Truck's Level 4 autonomous Freightliner Cascadia and launching cost-reduced InnovizThree sensor.

items.title: Spain's Largest Power Company Seeks Extended Nuclear Plant Operations
items.summary: Iberdrola plans to advocate for prolonging Spain's nuclear fleet operations amid a 5% year-on-year drop in nuclear production.
